concerns.description: Images missing or broken
concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a full-page infographic/flowchart on page 218 titled 'NORTH CAROLINA FISHERY MANAGEMENT PLANS May 2020' that outlines the steps and timeline for the Southern Flounder, Shrimp, and Blue Crab FMPs. In the HTML output, this page only contains the heading and the footer text; the flowchart itself is entirely missing (neither rendered as an image nor transcribed as text). This is a critical loss of meaningful visual information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Full-page flowchart/infographic outlining the 'NORTH CAROLINA FISHERY MANAGEMENT PLANS' process steps.
concerns.output_quote:
NORTH CAROLINA FISHERY MANAGEMENT PLANS
May 2020

State of North Carolina | Division of Marine Fisheries...

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The source document contains legislative text showing proposed rule amendments, which use strikethroughs for deletions and underlines for additions. The extraction captures both the deleted (strikethrough) and added (underlined) text contiguously as plain text without any markers. This fundamentally alters the meaning of the sentences, as both the removed words and the inserted words are read together as normal active text.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The following definitions shall apply [strikethrough: throughout] [underline: to] Section 18A .3400 of this Subchapter:
concerns.output_quote: The following definitions shall apply throughout to Section 18A .3400 of this Subchapter:

concerns.description: Lists converted incorrectly
concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains multiple Tables of Contents (e.g., pages 2, 3, 98, 159) that use dotted leaders and page numbers. In the HTML output, these page numbers are rendered exactly as they appear in the print document, which results in meaningless and confusing navigation for users since HTML represents a continuous scroll without page boundaries. The guidelines stipulate that retaining a source PDF TOC with dotted leader patterns and page number suffixes constitutes a major structural defect.

concerns.description: Heading hierarchy broken
concerns.analysis: The extraction inconsistently assigns heading levels to identically formatted section headers in the source PDF. On pages 68 through 72, the section headers "Literature Review", "Defining a circle hook", "Other Jurisdictions", and "Current circle hook regulation in North Carolina" all share the exact same visual formatting in the source PDF (Times New Roman, italicized, same size). However, in the output, these identically formatted headings are inconsistently classified as either H2 or H3.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Headings "Literature Review" (page 68), "Defining a circle hook" (page 69), "Other Jurisdictions" (page 71), and "Current circle hook regulation in North Carolina" (page 72) are all visually identical (italicized, same size).
concerns.output_quote: "Literature Review" is H3; "Defining a circle hook" is H2; "Other Jurisdictions" is H2; "Current circle hook regulation in North Carolina" is H3.

concerns.description: Content visually duplicated on page
concerns.analysis: On page 203 of the source PDF (Appendix A), there is a formal letter from NOAA. In the HTML output, the full text of this letter has been successfully extracted and transcribed (e.g., 'UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE...'). However, the HTML also includes multiple embedded images of the same letter (with alt text describing the letterhead and contents). This results in the user seeing the same letter content twice: once as transcribed text and again as embedded page screenshots.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Letter from NOAA to Braxton C. Davis dated 'JUL 19 2017'
concerns.output_quote:
The HTML contains the transcribed text '

UNITED STATES DEPARTMENT OF COMMERCE...

' alongside an image element: '

concerns.description: Missing table(s): source PDF has tables not found in output on p101(source:1,output:0), p211(source:1,output:0)
concerns.analysis: source_tables=3, output_tables=78. Pages with missing tables: p101(source:1,output:0), p211(source:1,output:0)

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 16 table(s) with matching structure across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis: split_table_count=16. Examples: pages 4-5: 2 cols (9:00 a.m., meeting overview – lara klibansky...); pages 125-126: 3 cols (year, date(s)...); pages 126-127: 3 cols (year, date(s)...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements. In HTML, these should be one continuous table to maintain accessibility and reading order.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 15 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=15. Examples: p57: "January"; p57: "February"; p57: "March". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Data row marked as header: 2 table(s) have numeric/data values in the header row
concerns.analysis: data_as_header=2. Examples: p205: [2009-2011, 2013-2015]; p205: [2009-2011, 2013-2015]. Header rows should contain column labels, not data values.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (8 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=8. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Missing images: 25 of 89 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=89, html_img_count=64, image_count_ratio=1.0449438202247192. Some source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 10 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=10. Examples: p1: "A serene coastal landscape at sunrise or sunset, f"; p17: "A presentation slide with a light blue background "; p29: "A document page featuring a large, irregularly sha". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 139 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=139, examples=['http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/02-2020-briefing-book', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=', 'http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_file?p_l_id=']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (255): Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda; 1; 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=255, pages_affected=245/248. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 19 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: [youtube.com; [portal.ncdenr.org; https://www.ncsbe.gov/Ethics/Education; https://EFILE.ncsbe.gov/; (http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/proclamation-m-19-2017).
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=19. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 9 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=9. Examples: p42: [portal.ncdenr.org; p212: Tina.Moore@ncdenr.gov; p212: Tina.Moore@ncdenr.gov. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: List broken by page break (1 instance(s)): p62→p63: list item ends “…eading of nesting habitat) of this long-”, continues as paragraph
concerns.analysis: split_list_count=1. Lists that span page breaks should be extracted as a single continuous structure. Split lists affect reading order and navigability for assistive technology.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "Rule Amendments:"; 18 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1, no_th_tables=18. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 1 form(s) with 2 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=1, total_fields=2. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 68 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (66 truncated mid-sentence, 2 generic placeholder(s)) — e.g. p1: "...r sunset, featuring calm water, marsh grass, and a"; p17: "...nd and a white header displaying the word "LETTERS"; p29: "...lock of black redaction covering text in the upper"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=68 (truncated=66, placeholder=2, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

concerns.description: Links displaced to end of page: 38 link(s) clustered at bottom of 14 page(s) instead of inline in text
concerns.analysis: displaced_link_pages=14, total_displaced=38. Links appear as standalone elements at the end of pages rather than being inline in the paragraph text.

concerns.description: Visual emphasis loss
concerns.analysis: The source document uses underlining heavily in its rulemaking sections (Pages 236-244) to indicate newly added legislative text. The extraction JSON completely loses all underline formatting, meaning there is no way for the reader to identify which parts of the text are the proposed additions.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: "Division" means the Division of Marine Fisheries or its authorized agent. (Underlined in source PDF)
concerns.output_quote: "Division" means the Division of Marine Fisheries or its authorized agent. (No underline notation in JSON)

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p81(source:4cols,output:2cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p81(source:4cols,output:2cols)

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "***Times indicated are merely for guidance. The co". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have time values in <th> cells: "9:00 a.m."
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=1, no_th_tables=18. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.

concerns.description: Source PDF contains 42 instance(s) of blue-styled text without hyperlink annotations. These appear to be intended as hyperlinks but were not annotated in the PDF (likely lost during Word-to-PDF export). This is a source document limitation, not a pipeline defect. Examples: page 6: "http://portal.ncdenr.org/web/mf/02-2020-briefing-b"; page 11: "http://portal.ncdenr.org/c/document_library/get_fi"; page 11: "e=DLFE-142450.pdf"; page 13: "e=DLFE-142457.pdf"; page 13: "e=DLFE-142458.pdf"
concerns.analysis: orphaned_blue_text_count=42. Blue text without link annotations detected via PyMuPDF color extraction.
